tldr: Four of your five live ads are wearing out, and two have been running unchanged for nearly three months. Every ad is the same swipeable format with the same emoji-heavy opener, so Meta sees them as basically one ad and quietly shows most of them to almost nobody.

benchmark.context: The typical brand your size posts a fresh ad every three weeks. The best ones do it every two. You have five live ads and they all look and sound alike, so Meta treats them as one and picks a favourite to show, which means you paid to make ads almost nobody sees.

ads.headline: 🐑 Wool Wadding with Natural Loft & Strength 💛
ads.body: All-purpose natural wadding for quilting, upholstery, padding & more - combining soft comfort with strong & lasting support 🌿 Crafted from wool, it naturally wicks moisture, offers fire-resistant properties, and is a sustainable choice designed to last over time 🔥🌾
drivers: This ad has been running unchanged for 87 days, three times longer than your ads should last.
The headline reads like a product page title, not a reason to stop scrolling in the feed.
Four emojis in the opening line signal 'ad' before anyone reads a single word.

ads.headline: Upgrade What You Sleep On 🛌🌿✨
ads.body: Whether you prefer soft or firm, low or high support, our range is designed to suit every sleep style, helping you stay aligned and comfortable throughout the night 🌙💫 Australian-made using microplastic-free, sustainable, and compostable materials 🌿💚
drivers: Also 87 days old, so the same tired ad is now competing with itself for the same shoppers.
The opening line says nothing specific, just 'upgrade' with no reason why or what changes.
The body lists features in the same 'made using X, Y, Z' shape as three of your other ads.

ads.headline: 60/40 Wool/Poly Wadding
drivers: The headline is a product code, not a reason to buy, so shoppers scroll past.
The body is one line and names three different products, so nobody knows what this ad is actually for.
43 days running with the weakest wording of any of your five ads, burning money slowly.

ads.headline: 🌿 Wool Weed Matting That Works Naturally 💚
ads.body: Take control of your soil with weed matting that reduces unwanted plant growth, improves moisture retention, and naturally enhances soil quality for long-term performance 🌿
drivers: Same leaf-emoji-headline-leaf-emoji pattern as two of your other live ads, so Meta groups them together.
Reads like a garden centre shelf label, no story, no customer, no before-and-after moment.
The garden buyer and the bedding buyer are different people, but this ad looks identical to your doona ads.

ads.headline: ✨Your Perfect Doona Awaits 🛌
ads.body: Experience a deeper, calmer, and more breathable sleep with organic wool bedding that naturally regulates temperature, feels incredibly soft, and supports your body night after night 🌿😴✨ Proudly Australian-made, our bedding is completely microplastic-free, offering a cleaner & more conscious way to rest while choosing a product that's both sustainable and fully compostable at the end of its long use💚
drivers: The 'microplastic-free, sustainable, compostable' line is copied almost word-for-word from your pillow ad.
The body is a 65-word paragraph on a phone screen, most shoppers stop reading after ten words.
'Your Perfect Doona Awaits' could belong to any bedding brand in Australia, nothing here is only yours.

hero_concept.headline: Coir. Rubber. Nothing else.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Most mattress toppers are foam. Foam is plastic. Plastic sheds microplastic into the room you sleep in for eight hours a night.

Our coir and natural rubber sheets are two things: coconut husk fibre and rubber tapped from a tree. That's the whole ingredient list. Firm support, breathable, and it will compost at the end of its life instead of sitting in landfill for 400 years.

Australian-made in the Adelaide Hills. Free shipping over $150.
hero_concept.visual_direction: A cross-section close-up of the coir and natural rubber sheet on a linen-covered bed, warm morning light from a window, showing the raw coconut fibre and rubber texture clearly.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your five live ads opens with an emoji and talks about product features. None of them tell a shopper why to care. This ad names the enemy (plastic in your bed) and puts your product on the other side of it, which is something none of your current ads do.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a calm bedroom scene with the sheet laid flat across a natural linen-covered bed, one corner folded back to reveal the raw coir and rubber cross-section, with soft morning window light falling across the fibres from the left. Warm, natural, Australian-farmhouse aesthetic with muted earth tones, unbleached linen, and a wooden bedside frame just visible at the edge. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact natural coir fibre colour (warm coconut brown) and natural rubber layer (pale cream-tan) exactly as shown in the reference; strictly NO dyed colours, NO grey shift, NO orange or red-brown shift, NO bleaching to white, NO synthetic foam substitution. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction: flat rectangular sheet with visible layered structure of coir fibre bonded to natural rubber backing, same thickness ratio and same fibre density as shown in the reference, exposed cross-section at the folded corner showing both layers clearly. Do NOT add stitching, do NOT add a fabric cover, do NOT add piping or edging, do NOT change the sheet into a mattress or pillow, do NOT alter the layer thickness or fibre pattern. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Your two 87-day-old ads are done inside a week, and the wadding ad follows two weeks after that.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Wool Valley refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Colita, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads: the pictures, the wording, and how long each has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
